occupation_lookup.title: Electronics Mechanics and Servicers
occupation_lookup.notes: Phone repair, device servicing — dominant in urban Ghana/Nigeria
occupation_lookup.risk_explanation: This occupation shows low automation exposure (26%) in NGA. Skills involving judgment, care, and physical dexterity are harder to automate.
occupation_lookup.wage_caveat: Formal sector median. Informal sector wages typically 30-60% lower.

occupation_lookup.title: Shopkeepers and Stall Keepers
occupation_lookup.notes: Market traders, small retail — largest urban informal sector
occupation_lookup.risk_explanation: Some tasks in this work (62% probability) could be automated, but hands-on, relationship-based aspects are more durable.
durable_skills: Advanced technical skills in your trade
adjacent_upskill: Digital marketing and social media selling

econometric_signal_1_wages.title: Monthly earnings by sector — Nigeria (formal sector)
econometric_signal_1_wages.source: Converted from NGN at 2022 parallel market rate (~700 NGN/USD). Official rate understates real purchasing power loss.
econometric_signal_1_wages.caveat: Formal sector only. Converted at 2022 parallel market rate. Informal sector wages typically 20-50% lower.

econometric_signal_2_growth.title: Sector growth outlook — Nigeria
econometric_signal_2_growth.source: World Bank Nigeria Economic Update 2023; ILO Nigeria Country Brief; PwC Nigeria Economic Outlook 2024
econometric_signal_2_growth.caveat: Forward-looking estimate. Nigeria's economy is volatile — monitor FX and oil price movements.

growing.rationale: Lagos tech ecosystem (Yaba, Lekki) — fastest growing in SSA. Andela, Flutterwave, Paystack effect.
Post-CBN liberalisation: OPay, PalmPay, Moniepoint growing agent networks rapidly
Afrobeats global expansion; streaming revenue; formal employment growth in content
Jumia, Konga, informal WhatsApp commerce expansion
Population growth driving demand; significant undersupply

at_risk.rationale: Automation of routine office tasks. BPO sector under pressure.
ATM and mobile banking reducing teller demand

wittgenstein_chart.title: Education levels for young people (15-29) in Nigeria
wittgenstein_chart.subtitle: Where your region is heading by 2035
wittgenstein_chart.source: Wittgenstein Centre for Demography and Global Human Capital (2018)
wittgenstein_chart.key_trend: Nigeria has much larger no-education and primary-only population than Ghana. By 2035, meaningful improvement projected but slower than Ghana trajectory. Massive youth cohort means absolute numbers of out-of-school youth remain high even as percentages improve.

youth_neet_pct.note: Significantly higher than Ghana. Reflects large northern states with low female education participation

informal_employment_pct.note: One of the highest informal employment shares globally

gdp_per_capita_usd.note: Sharp decline from 2014 peak of $3222 due to oil price crash and naira depreciation

human_capital_index.note: Significantly below Ghana (0.45). Large gap between Lagos/Abuja and north.
